channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iraq Celebrate World Cup Qualification Amid War Tension
item.description: For Baghdad, happiness burst through the city as Iraq national football team secure a spot for the World Cup for the first time since 1986. After a 2-1 victory over Bolivia, celebration scatter the ca...

item.title: Estate agents launch legal action over high Rightmove fees
item.description: Several estate agents in the UK have launched a class action against Rightmove, the leading online property portal, alleging that the platform charges 'unsustainable' subscription fees. The legal acti...

item.title: Government to Start Upgrading Singida Water Institute Campus from 2026/2027
item.description: The Tanzanian government, through the Deputy Minister for Water, Kundo Mathew, has announced plans to start phased construction of buildings at the Water Institute located in Singida. The construction...

item.title: Marrying Millions Cast: 2026 Net Worth Rankings and Relationship Updates
item.description: The reality TV show 'Marrying Millions' showcases couples from diverse financial backgrounds, exploring whether love or money keeps them together. As of 2026, the cast's net worth ranges from $10 mill...

item.title: Illegal online casino network exploiting vulnerable UK gamblers uncovered
item.description: An investigation by The Guardian and Investigate Europe has uncovered a sprawling international network of illegal online casinos that have been targeting British gamblers. Operating through opaque ju...

item.title: Fuel price hikes cause major drop in Baguio tourism arrivals
item.description: Baguio City’s tourism industry is experiencing a serious slowdown, with local officials confirming that tourist arrivals have fallen by 40 to 50 percent due to the continued rise in fuel prices. Mayor...

item.title: Foxtel announces further technical job cuts following DAZN merger
item.description: Foxtel has confirmed another round of job cuts, shedding at least 20 technical roles as it continues to merge with British streaming giant DAZN. Employees were informed via email on Wednesday, describ...

item.title: Four police officers injured in Lakki Marwat explosion
item.description: In Lakki Marwat district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, four police officers sustained injuries in an explosion on Wednesday in Serai Naurang town. Police spokesperson Qudratullah Khan confirmed to Dawn that ...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Mini Crossword Update
item.description: The Age has published its Superquiz for Thursday, April 2, 2026, inviting readers to engage with a series of trivia questions. The article also highlights the availability of a Mini Crossword featurin...

item.title: Taylor Lorenz Defends Her Always-Online Lifestyle and Digital Habits
item.description: In an in-depth profile for WIRED’s new 'User Behavior' series, journalist and content creator Taylor Lorenz discusses her extremely online lifestyle, proudly clocking nearly 17 hours of screen time pe...

item.title: Considerations and Safety for a Second Brazilian Butt Lift Procedure
item.description: The Brazilian Butt Lift (BBL) continues to be a popular cosmetic procedure worldwide, but some women consider a second surgery to improve results. Dr. Muriu Kinuthia, a plastic, reconstructive, and ae...

item.title: Canada's Manufacturing Output Slows as Tariffs and Middle East Conflict Raise Costs
item.description: Canada's manufacturing sector showed signs of stagnation in March, as reflected by the S&P Global Canada Manufacturing Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI), which fell to 50.0 from 51.0 in February, marki...

item.title: Tinubu Reaffirms Support for Para Athletes at National Games Opening in Abuja
item.description: The third edition of the National Para Games kicked off in Abuja with vibrant cultural displays, dances, and acrobatic performances at the Moshood Abiola National Stadium. The colourful ceremony celeb...

item.title: South Africa Faces Persistent Challenges in Prosecuting Water and Sanitation Corruption
item.description: Since 2008, the Special Investigating Unit (SIU) has completed nine of sixteen investigations into fraud and corruption in South Africa's water and sanitation sector, covering over R6.2-billion and re...

item.title: NFL referee alleges gender discrimination and unfair treatment in workplace lawsuit
item.description: Robin DeLorenzo, one of the NFL’s pioneering female referees, has filed a harassment lawsuit against the league, alleging three years of gender-based discrimination, humiliation, and hostility. Accord...

item.title: Nunavut prepares for next year’s transfer of land and resource control from Ottawa
item.description: Nunavut is entering its final year before devolution takes full effect in April 2027, when responsibility for managing the territory’s land and natural resources will transfer from the federal governm...

item.title: Philippines Requests Safe Passage for Its Ships from Iran Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The Philippines has formally requested Iran to recognize it as a “non-hostile country” and to guarantee safe passage for Philippine-flagged vessels and oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. This...

item.title: The Role of Russia, India, and China in Shaping Asia’s Emerging Multipolar Order
item.description: The global system is now passing a turning point where the unipolar dominance that once existed has ended, giving way to a fluid and often unstable multipolar world. In this shifting environment, the ...

item.title: NHAI Implements Full Digital Payment System at Toll Plazas Starting 1 April
item.description: From 1 April onwards, the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has announced that all toll plazas across the country will completely shift to digital payments, ending the use of cash. This majo...

item.title: Akpabio Hails Tinubu at 74, Calls Him Bold and Visionary Leader
item.description: The President of the Nigerian Senate, Godswill Akpabio, don show plenty respect to President Bola Ahmed Tinubu as the President clock 74 years old. For him message wey he personally sign, Akpabio call...
